macro_segments.title: War, Famine, and Chaotic Circumstances: When a Woman's Report About Her Husband's Death Is Unreliable

micro_segments.title: Introduction: The Woman's Credibility in Times of Peace vs. War
Rava's Distinction: Famine (Ra'avon) Is Worse Than War
Related Chaotic Situations: Building Collapse, Snakes and Scorpions, Pestilence
The Dispute on Pestilence (Dever): Does It Create the Same Concern as War?

macro_segments.title: The Migo Argument: Does the Woman's Choice to Report War Strengthen Her Credibility?

micro_segments.title: The Question Raised (Iba'ya Lehu): Did the Woman Have to Report That War Existed?
The Migo Principle: Why Reporting War Doesn't Help If She's Drawing Wrong Conclusions
Tosafot's Point: The Woman Always Has a Migo (She Could Have Said He Died Naturally)

macro_segments.title: Cases Where the Woman Is Not Believed: House/Cave Filled with Smoke

micro_segments.title: The Baraita: Woman Reports House/Cave Filled with Smoke and Husband Died
Rashi Explains: The Miraculous Escape from Smoke Shows Others Can Survive Too

macro_segments.title: Cases Where the Woman Is Believed: Attack by Non-Jews or Bandits with Her Escape

micro_segments.title: The Baraita: Non-Jews or Bandits Attacked; Husband Died, Wife Escaped
Why She Is Believed: Rav Idi's Principle—'A Woman's Weapons Are Upon Her'
Two Reasons for Belief: Unique Incident to Them, Plus Explanation for Why She Survived

macro_segments.title: The Wedding Canopy Fire: Distinguishing Between Concrete Evidence and Assumptions

micro_segments.title: The Story: Fire Under the Wedding Canopy; Wife Calls for Help; Charred Body Found
Rav Chiyya's Attempted Comparison to the Smoke Case (Rejected)
Rava's Distinction: Unlike Smoke, Here She Calls Witnesses and a Charred Body Is Found
Rav Chiyya's Counter-Theory: The Body Might Be Someone Else; The Hand Might Be Disfigurement

macro_segments.title: The Question of a Single Male Witness (Ed Echad) in War: Is He Believed?

micro_segments.title: The Question Posed: Is a Third-Party Witness Believed in a War Situation?
Two Reasons to Believe a Single Witness: Facts Eventually Become Known; Woman's Own Investigation
Argument Against Belief: She Won't Investigate if Based on Perception (Amra Bedidami)

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Akiva's Report from Nehardea: Acceptance of One Witness in War at Time of Troops

micro_segments.title: The Story: Rabbi Akiva in Nehardea; Nechemia Reports the Halakha About One Witness
The Question: Is This About War or About General Acceptance of One Witness?
Rava's Reinterpretation: The Mention of Troops Refers to Travel Difficulty, Not War Halakha

macro_segments.title: The Drowned Scholars: Women Witnesses and the Requirement for Bodily Marks (Simanim)

micro_segments.title: The Story: Two Torah Sages Drowned with Abba Yossi ben Simai; Wives Allowed to Remarry on Women's Testimony
Water as War: The Presumption That Bodies Might Not Be Identifiable
The Problem: Water Without End—How Do We Know He Didn't Survive?
The Resolution: The Witnesses Must Identify the Body by Specific Bodily Marks (Simanim)

macro_segments.title: The Sesame Seeds Analogy: Can We Assume Identical Amounts Refer to the Same Object?

micro_segments.title: The Story: Man Claims Friend Returned His Sesame Seeds; Friend Claims They Are Different Ones
Rav Chisda's Attempted Analogy: Like the Drowned Scholars, Same Amount = Same Object
Rava's Objection: The Drowned Case Required Simanim; Seeds Have No Identifying Marks
The Gemara's Response: Equal Weight Is Not Sufficient as an Identifying Mark

macro_segments.title: When Can We Assume a Vessel's Contents Are Unchanged? The Case of Labeled Vessels (Terumah)

micro_segments.title: The Mishna on Vessel Labels: Kuf (Korban), Mem (Maaser), Dalet (Dimua), Tet (Tevel), Tav (Terumah)
The Principle: We Assume Labeled Vessels Were Not Emptied and Reused
Rabbi Yosei's Exception: Even a Labeled Barrel of Terumah Can Be Assumed Empty and Reused
The Dispute: Does Leaving the Label Mean the Vessel Was Not Emptied?
Application to Sesame Seeds: Unlabeled Barrels Can Reasonably Be Assumed Emptied and Reused

macro_segments.title: Yitzchak Reish Galuta's Death: Are We Concerned About Multiple People with the Same Name?

micro_segments.title: The Story: Yitzchak, Reish Galuta, Son of Rabbi Bibi's Sister, Dies While Traveling to Spain
The Question: Do We Worry There Might Be Two People with the Same Identifying Details?
Abaye's Position: We Should Be Concerned About Multiple People with the Same Name
Rava's Position: We Are Not Concerned Once Someone Fits Those Specific Details

macro_segments.title: Rava's Proof: The Case of the Get (Divorce Document) Signed by Androlinai

micro_segments.title: The Story: A Get Is Found Signed by 'Androlinai,' Husband of Sarah from Nehardea
The Question: Must We Investigate the Entire Region to Ensure No One Else Has These Names?
Rav Yehuda Nesi'ah's Response: Check the Entire Nehardea for Others with Matching Names
Rava's Critique: This Would Require Checking the Entire World; Rav Yehuda Likely Meant to Honor

macro_segments.title: Two Documents with Mirror-Image Names: Can We Identify the Debtor Without Concern for Duplicates?

micro_segments.title: The Story: Two IOUs from Mechoza with Names 'Bar Nigai' and 'Ninai bar Chavi'
Rava bar Avuha's Collection: He Collected Using These IOUs Despite Name Confusion
Rashi and Rava's Conclusion: Context and Circumstances Allow Us to Identify the Right Parties

macro_segments.title: Abaye's Counter-Argument: Why the Creditor Wouldn't Have Dropped the IOU or Given It to Someone with the Same Name

micro_segments.title: Three Reasons Not to Worry: Drop Risk (No), Safekeeping Risk (No), Transfer Risk (No)
Why Creditors Wouldn't Entrust to Someone with Identical Name; Why We Don't Fear the IOU Was Transferred Without Full Acquisition

macro_segments.title: Summary and Broader Implications: Drawing Conclusions Based on Context and Identifying Marks

micro_segments.title: Spectrum of Cases: From High Suspicion (Reish Galuta) to Routine Confidence (IOUs)
The Parallel to Amra Bidedami: We Often Must Connect Dots, Not Just for Bodies but for Names and Identities
The Unresolved Problem: Why Don't We Simply Ask More Questions Rather Than Ban the Woman's Testimony?

topical_tags.term: Amra Bidedami (She Says Based on Perception)
Rabban Gamliel Hazaken (Rabban Gamliel the Elder)
Abaye vs. Rava Dispute (on Name Identification)
